steps.voice: The Display card has four controls — brightness, dimmer-switch binding, night mode, and screen rotation. Three of these actually work here in the sandbox, so play with them after the tour.
Brightness slider — five to one hundred percent. In the real device this drives the backlight LED via PWM. In the sandbox it dims the entire canvas so you can see the effect.

Dimmer Switch Config lets you bind brightness to a CAN signal — so your headlight switch automatically drops the dash to a comfortable level at night. You set a signal, a threshold, and a dim level. Momentary or persistent, your choice.
Night Mode is the manual version. Tap it to toggle — the dash shifts to a dimmer colour palette and every widget with a Night Override applies its night-time colours.
You configure night colours per widget — same label, different palette. So your coolant panel can go from bright green during the day to dim red at night, without losing readability.
Rotation cycles zero, ninety, one-eighty, two-seventy. Useful if you've mounted the dash sideways or upside-down in an unusual dash pod.
All four settings persist to NVS, so they survive power cycles. The dash boots up exactly how you left it.
